coreConcept: The water cycle is the journey water takes as it evaporates, travels through the atmosphere, and returns as precipitation. It helps provide the fresh water necessary for all living things.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: The Sun
keyActorsAndComponents.description: The Sun heats up the water in rivers, lakes, and oceans, causing it to turn into vapor and rise into the air.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: Clouds
keyActorsAndComponents.description: Clouds are made of tiny water droplets and they carry water vapor around the earth until it’s heavy enough to fall back down.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: Rain
keyActorsAndComponents.description: Rain is the water that falls from clouds when they become too full. It replenishes rivers, lakes, and the soil.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: Groundwater
keyActorsAndComponents.description: Groundwater is water that seeps into the ground and is stored in rocks and soil, helping plants grow.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: Plants
keyActorsAndComponents.description: Plants take in water through their roots and release it back into the air through a process called transpiration.

storyOfProcess.name: Evaporation
storyOfProcess.description: Water from oceans, rivers, and lakes is heated by the Sun and turns into vapor, rising into the atmosphere.

storyOfProcess.name: Condensation
storyOfProcess.description: As water vapor rises, it cools and turns back into tiny droplets, forming clouds.

storyOfProcess.name: Precipitation
storyOfProcess.description: When the clouds get heavy with water droplets, they release the water as rain, snow, or sleet, falling back to the ground.

storyOfProcess.name: Collection
storyOfProcess.description: The fallen water collects in rivers, lakes, and underground, starting the cycle all over again.

corePrinciples.name: Energy Transfer
corePrinciples.description: The Sun's energy is essential to drive the water cycle and maintain temperatures for life on Earth.

corePrinciples.name: Water Conservation
corePrinciples.description: Understanding the water cycle helps us appreciate and conserve our water resources that are vital for survival.

corePrinciples.name: Ecosystem Interactions
corePrinciples.description: The water cycle supports diverse ecosystems by providing necessary hydration for plants and animals.
